tier_remaining_note: These four fields drift from the live generate_build_plan_v2.py scoring model (a fresh recount on 2026-07-16, run with a corrected non-Windows ROUTES_DIR path, found A=12, B=621 remaining before this chunk's 50 routes) and are not covered by verify_build_state.py's disk reconciliation, only route_pairs/blog/total_markdown_files are. tier_b_remaining above is only decremented by this chunk's 50 routes from that 2026-07-16 recount, not recomputed from scratch each run. A future maintenance pass should fix ROUTES_DIR in generate_build_plan_v2.py (currently a Windows-style backslash path that silently finds 0 built routes on Linux) and add tier-remaining reconciliation to verify_build_state.py so these four fields stop drifting silently.
notes: Chunks 85-86 DONE (Template E + Template A, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 12 parallel research agents, one per country/cluster, selected from a disk-reconciled rerun of generate_build_plan_v2.py's scoring model (fixed its Windows-style ROUTES_DIR path locally to recount correctly on Linux: 6,571 built, Tier A=12, Tier B=621 remaining before this chunk) rather than manual reciprocal-gap spotting alone this time, taking the 50 highest-scored unbuilt Tier B pairs. Countries covered: Laos, Seychelles, Trinidad and Tobago (each gained their missing Canada leg, both directions, closing out Canada coverage alongside chunks 81-83's Brunei/Uzbekistan work); Iceland (brand new country on the site, zero prior pages: Germany and France both directions plus Singapore both directions, 6 routes); Barbados (United States both directions plus Australia both directions, 4 routes, extending chunk 82-83's UK/UAE-only coverage); Belize (brand new country, 7 routes: UK and US both directions, Australia both directions, Belize-to-UAE one direction); Botswana (brand new country, 7 routes: same pattern as Belize); Guyana and Namibia (each gained US and Australia both directions, 4 routes each, extending chunk 82-83's UK/UAE-only coverage); Suriname (brand new country, 6 routes: UK and US both directions, Australia both directions); Zambia (US both directions, 2 routes, extending chunk 82-83's UK/UAE-only coverage); Macau (Germany both directions, 2 routes, extending its existing Australia/Canada/France/UK/US coverage); Venezuela (Canada both directions, 2 routes, closing the gap chunk 84 deliberately deferred to hold that batch at 50). Genuine differentiators found: Iceland's MAST enforces a mandatory 14-day quarantine at one of two approved isolation stations for every incoming pet regardless of EU/EEA origin or pet-passport status, confirmed by direct fetch of mast.is; this directly contradicts the site's existing iceland-pet-import-guide.md blog post and icelandair.md airline page, both of which currently claim no mandatory quarantine, an inconsistency flagged for a future correction pass but not fixed this run since those files are outside this batch's scope. Singapore's AVS places Iceland in Schedule II (Controlled Risk) rather than the negligible-risk Schedule I reserved for Australia/NZ/Ireland/UK, so a rabies-free country still needs a titre test and conditional quarantine into Singapore, while MAST's own Category 1 list treats Singapore as low-risk in the reverse direction, a genuine sourced asymmetry between the two regulators. Icelandair stopped carrying pets as checked passenger-hold baggage from 1 November 2024, so all Iceland routes now describe manifested cargo movement, not hold travel. Belize and Botswana each needed their pet-import/export authority identified from scratch (BAHA for Belize; Botswana's Department of Veterinary Services, DVS, under the Ministry of Agriculture, not the Department of Animal Health and Production named on the site's existing air-botswana.md airline page); Belize surfaced a live 2024-2025 New World Screwworm re-emergence (screwworm-free since 1995 until livestock cases returned in December 2024 and a human case in August 2025), triggering a US-bound screwworm inspection certificate layered on the CDC high-risk pathway. Barbados was independently verified as DAFF Group 2 (rabies-free tier, no vaccination or RNATT titre test at all), a genuine contrast with Trinidad and Tobago, Jamaica, the Bahamas and Bermuda, all Group 3 with the titre test and 180-day wait. Guyana's own GLDA authority does not treat the United States as rabies-free on entry despite the CDC's own rabies-controlled classification of the US, so a US-origin pet faces the same titre-test pathway as a UAE-origin one. Venezuela's Simon Bolivar International Airport (Caracas) closure was independently re-verified today, 16 July 2026, rather than copied forward from chunk 84: still closed to scheduled commercial traffic, with aviation sources still expecting several months before normal operations resume and Avianca/Copa still running the Valencia contingency routing. QA pass after the agents finished caught one systematic issue: belize-to-united-states.md and united-states-to-belize.md were written with template_variant 'A' instead of this batch's assigned 'E' for that pair, corrected across both files before commit with a scoped two-file fix. All 50 files QA-scanned clean after that fix (zero em dashes; zero banned vocabulary; correct Marcus Webb persona on all 50; valid keyed-map YAML for every requirements block verified with a Python parser; all route_data fields populated; route_complexity restricted to the valid low/moderate/high/very_high tokens; every sideways/upward link verified against real files on disk with a Python script, zero broken links; all 50 titles confirmed unique; slug fields matched filenames on all 50; opening sentences independently varied and fact-led, no templated 'Moving a pet from X to Y' skeleton on any of the 50; government-authority email/phone contact details cited on a handful of pages, e.g. Botswana DVS and Seychelles NBA, are legitimate sourced regulator contacts, not PetTransportGlobal's own, consistent with the precedent already set in chunk 82-83's notes). Also flagged, not fixed this run: several existing Trinidad and Tobago sibling pages link to a non-existent /pet-transport/countries/trinidad-and-tobago/ page; the new trinidad-and-tobago-to-canada.md and canada-to-trinidad-and-tobago.md pages correctly avoid that broken target. Routes 6,571 -> 6,621, pages 7,377 -> 7,427. Chunk 87 (Template B, Tier B) is next.
_previous_notes_chunk_84: Chunk 84 DONE (Template D, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Closed the Australia/UAE/Canada reciprocal gap for the 12-country Tier B cluster opened by the UK (chunk 80), US (chunk 81) and partial Canada/UAE (chunks 82-83) runs: Bolivia, El Salvador, Fiji, Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua, Paraguay, Russia, Serbia, Tunisia, Ukraine and Venezuela. Fiji was independently verified as DAFF-approved (Group 2) and MOCCAE low-risk-listed while still carrying its own mandatory BAF entry quarantine. Serbia is DAFF Group 3 approved despite Air Serbia excluding pets from its Belgrade-Dubai and Belgrade-UK routes. Russia-UAE reopened via Aeroflot/FlyDubai after a partial ceasefire; Ukraine still routes overland to Poland. Venezuela's Caracas airport earthquake closure (24 June 2026) was re-verified as still in effect. Routes 6,521 -> 6,571, pages 7,327 -> 7,377. Chunk 85 (Template E, Tier B) is next.
_previous_notes_chunk_82_83: Chunks 82-83 DONE (Template B + Template C, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 14 parallel research agents. Closed reciprocal-pair gaps across three hub clusters: CANADA cluster (CFIA rules) with Brunei, Uzbekistan, Honduras, Nicaragua, Paraguay, Tunisia and Ukraine (canada-to-brunei was the sole missing direction after brunei-to-canada shipped in chunk 81; uzbekistan, honduras, nicaragua, paraguay, tunisia and ukraine each got both directions with Canada); UAE cluster (MOCCAE rules) with Honduras, Nicaragua, Paraguay, Tunisia, Ukraine, Barbados, Guyana, Namibia and Zambia (both directions each); UK cluster (DEFRA/APHA rules) with Barbados, Guyana, Namibia, Zambia, Albania and Algeria (both directions each) plus Andorra and Bahamas (both directions) and a single Belarus-to-UK direction (UK-to-Belarus intentionally left for a future chunk); and a standalone France-Macau pair (both directions). Barbados, Guyana, Namibia, Zambia, Albania, Algeria, Andorra and Belarus each received their first route coverage on the site (previously zero pages). Genuine differentiators found: Brunei imposes quarantine on Canadian-origin pets regardless of paperwork (14 days with a passing titre test, 30 days without) because Canada isn't on Brunei's short rabies-free list, and Royal Brunei Airlines doesn't serve North America at all, so routing runs via Cathay Pacific Cargo or Malaysia Airlines Cargo (Singapore Airlines Cargo explicitly excludes Brunei from its live-animal network). CFIA's high-risk-for-dog-rabies list (dated 13 March 2025) covers Uzbekistan, Honduras, Tunisia and Ukraine but not Nicaragua or Paraguay; the resulting CFIA ban (since 28 June/28 September 2022 depending on species date) applies to commercial dogs only (resale, adoption, fostering, breeding, show, research), never an owner-accompanied personal pet, a distinction confirmed and stated explicitly across every Canada-side high-risk-country page this batch. MOCCAE requires a FAVN/RNATT titre test for every one of these origin countries except confirmation was inconclusive for Barbados (flagged as needing direct confirmation per the YMYL guard rather than guessed), and MOCCAE imposes no fixed post-titre waiting period the way the EU/UK do, making several UAE-bound routes faster than their UK counterparts despite both needing a titre test. Ukraine's civilian airspace remains closed as of mid-2026 (confirmed against EASA's Conflict Zone Information Bulletin and Ukraine's July 2026 Civil Aviation Development Strategy), so all four Ukraine pages route overland to Poland or Romania before any flight. Belarus is Part 2 listed by the UK (no titre test needed, same category as the US/Canada) but the EU/UK airspace ban on Belarusian carriers, imposed after the May 2021 Ryanair Flight 4978 forced landing and expanded again in December 2025, remains fully in force, so routing requires an overland transfer to Vilnius or Warsaw before flying. Andorra has no commercial airport, so both Andorra-UK pages route overland via Barcelona or Toulouse; Andorra is GB Part 1 listed (no titre test). Barbados is GB Part 2 listed and requires a Barbados-side non-stop Monday-to-Friday London flight per its own import conditions (APHA export certificate 3890), currently only satisfied by British Airways. Guyana is unlisted for GB entry but is one of only 11 countries Guyana's own GLDA authority treats as rabies-free for its own imports, including the UK; Guyana also sits on the CDC's high-risk list, which blocks American Airlines Cargo dog bookings originating there, ruling out a Miami/JFK connection for dogs (not cats) on Guyana-outbound routes. Namibia has no domestic FAVN lab, so titre blood samples route to the Onderstepoort Veterinary Institute in South Africa. Zambia has a named GB export certificate, EHC 3928 (version 3, January 2024 guidance), and a genuine nonstop Emirates Lusaka-Dubai service (EK713/EK714) despite no nonstop Lusaka-London route existing. Tunisia-UAE is one of the few genuinely direct routes on the site (Tunisair and Emirates both fly Tunis-Dubai nonstop); Tunisia's existing Pit Bull/Rottweiler/Tosa/Mastiff-type breed ban was confirmed as an import-side rule only, so it doesn't block those breeds leaving Tunisia for Canada. Macau-France found that Macau (unlike Hong Kong) is not on the EU's rabies-risk-exempt list under the Delegated Regulation (EU) 2026/131 that took effect 22 April 2026, so a Macau-origin pet needs the full titre test and 90-day wait into France despite Hong Kong next door being exempt; Macau-side facts were cross-checked against the already-published canada-to-macau.md and united-kingdom-to-macau.md pages for consistency before writing, no discrepancies found. All 50 files QA-scanned clean (zero em dashes, zero banned vocabulary after manual review of initial 'embark'/'foster' substring hits, which were all legitimate uses: American Airlines' PetEmbark cargo programme, animal 'fostering' in the specific adoption sense, and 'embarkation' as a genuine travel term, not the banned figurative usage; correct Marcus Webb persona on all 50; valid keyed-map YAML for every requirements block verified with a Python parser; all route_data fields populated; every sideways/upward link verified against real files on disk with a Python script, zero broken links found; opening sentences independently varied and fact-led, no templated 'Moving a pet from X to Y' skeleton on any of the 50). Routes 6,471 -> 6,521, pages 7,277 -> 7,327. Chunk 84 (Template D, Tier B) is next. Content plan Day 32 (UK to South Africa route page) reconciliation check remains outstanding, not addressed this run.

counts.note: total_site_pages counts content .md source files. The full deployed total (including Hugo taxonomy and list pages) is verified from sitemap.xml after a build.

counts_method: Reconciled from disk by verify_build_state.py. Never hand-edit count fields; run 'python verify_build_state.py --write' instead.
_dropped_from_rolling_window: Chunk 80 and chunk 81 notes were dropped 2026-07-16 to cap file growth (git history still has them in full at commits 4ecac2f1 and 3741f752). A rolling window of the two most recent previous-chunk notes (currently chunk 84 and chunks 82-83) is kept going forward.
